title: engine: build status.py (queue depth/age/recent outcomes) and generalize VERIFIERS beyond one hardcoded ticket kind
prompt:
The engine (engine/orchestrator.py, engine/store/) has a genuinely good ticket schema (queue_id, requested_by, verify_kind, claimed_by/claimed_at, audit.jsonl's ticket->worker->session_id chain) but zero aggregation — answering 'what's queued, how old is the oldest ticket, did ticket X succeed' today means ls-ing store/queue/ and store/done/ and reading raw JSON by hand. Separately, orchestrator.py's VERIFIERS dict (orchestrator.py:217) has exactly ONE entry (verify_constellation_4_apps) — every other ticket kind, including the 8 tickets this evaluation is filing right now, has no automated verification path; only the worker's own self-report in results/<ticket>.json is checked.

Two pieces:

1. Build engine/status.py: prints queue depth, oldest queued ticket's age, and the last N done tickets with title + result/verified outcome, reading store/queue/, store/done/, and audit.jsonl. No new state — pure read/aggregate over the existing ledger.

2. Generalize VERIFIERS: add at minimum one generic, declarative verifier (e.g. a ticket can carry a `verify_spec: {files_changed: [...], must_contain: [...]}` block, checked by a single generic function) so a new ticket kind doesn't require hand-writing a bespoke Python function in orchestrator.py every time. Keep the existing verify_constellation_4_apps working unchanged; this is additive.
verify: python3 engine/status.py prints a correct queue depth and oldest-ticket age matching a manual ls/cat of store/queue/; VERIFIERS has more than one working entry, demonstrated against at least one of this evaluation's own 8 tickets once it reaches done/.
